---
title: "about Me"
---
I hold a Master’s degree in Epidemiology and Biostatistics from the University of California, San Francisco (UCSF) and have over five years of experience applying statistical methods to real-world clinical and public health data. I work closely with cross-functional teams—including clinicians, researchers, and program stakeholders—to support data-driven decision-making across the research lifecycle.

My expertise includes developing analytic strategies, conducting high-quality statistical analyses, and translating complex results into clear, actionable insights for both technical and non-technical audiences. I have a strong track record of delivering reproducible analyses that inform operational strategy, program evaluation, and evidence-based initiatives, with a focus on clarity, efficiency, and impact.
